channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Amazon chips no longer just a side dish, they're a $20B biz
item.description: Amazon's semiconductor business has surpassed a $20 billion annual run rate, positioning it among the top three datacenter chip companies globally. CEO Andy Jassy indicated that the revenue could approach $50 billion if Amazon included itself as a customer.

item.title: How Elon Musk Squeezed OpenAI: They 'Are Gonna Want to Kill Me’
item.description: Tensions escalated in the trial involving Elon Musk and OpenAI as Musk faced cross-examination from OpenAI's lawyers. The trial has drawn significant attention due to the high-profile nature of the parties involved.

item.title: Microsoft says it has over 20M paid Copilot users, and they really are using it
item.description: Microsoft announced that it has over 20 million paid users for its Copilot service, countering perceptions of low engagement. The company highlighted growing user activity and satisfaction.

item.title: Google Cloud surpasses $20B, but says growth was capacity-constrained
item.description: Google Cloud achieved over $20 billion in quarterly revenue for the first time, driven by high demand for AI services. However, growth was limited by capacity constraints.

item.title: Eero Signal keeps your business online during internet outages
item.description: Eero Signal is a new cellular backup device that connects to Eero's mesh Wi-Fi system, providing internet continuity during outages. This innovation aims to minimize downtime for businesses.

item.title: Extracting contract insights with PwC’s AI-driven annotation on AWS
item.description: PwC has developed an AI-driven annotation tool on AWS to streamline contract analysis, helping legal and compliance teams extract insights from lengthy documents more efficiently.

item.title: Google gains 25M subscriptions in Q1, driven by YouTube and Google One
item.description: Google added 25 million paid subscriptions in Q1, reaching a total of 350 million, largely driven by growth in YouTube and Google One services.

item.title: Emergency First Responders Say Waymos Are Getting Worse
item.description: Emergency responders have expressed concerns that Waymo's autonomous vehicles are becoming more problematic, suggesting that the technology was deployed prematurely and in excessive numbers.

item.title: Taylor Swift Wants to Trademark Her Likeness. These TikTok Deepfake Ads Show Why
item.description: Researchers have highlighted the misuse of AI-generated deepfake ads featuring celebrities like Taylor Swift to deceive users into sharing personal information, prompting discussions on digital rights.

item.title: Researchers move in the right direction, develop powerful GPS interference alarm
item.description: Researchers at Oak Ridge National Laboratory have developed a portable detector kit that can effectively identify real GPS signals from spoofed ones, addressing serious concerns about GPS interference.

item.title: Organizing Agents’ memory at scale: Namespace design patterns in AgentCore Memory
item.description: The article discusses designing namespace hierarchies and retrieval patterns for AgentCore Memory, including implementing AWS IAM-based access control. This guidance is crucial for efficiently managing memory in AI agents.

item.title: Motorola Razr Ultra (2026) vs. Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 7: I tried both, and there's a clear winner
item.description: A comparison between the Motorola Razr Ultra and Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 7 reveals that Samsung excels in durability and AI features, while Motorola focuses on hardware. The review highlights the strengths and weaknesses of both devices.

item.title: Microsoft's patch for a 0-day exploited by Russian spies fell short. Another Windows flaw is under attack
item.description: Microsoft and CISA have alerted users about a zero-click Windows flaw being exploited by Russian spies, indicating a significant security risk. The patch for a previous vulnerability was insufficient, raising concerns about ongoing threats.

item.title: Is AI video just a prequel? Runway’s CEO thinks world models are next
item.description: Runway's CEO discusses the rapid evolution of AI-generated video and the company's significant funding, positioning it against top competitors like Google and OpenAI. The focus is shifting towards developing advanced world models.

item.title: Forget Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra: I found alternatives that are nearly as good for less money
item.description: The article suggests that last year's smartphone models from Google, Samsung, and Motorola offer nearly the same performance as the latest Galaxy S26 Ultra at a lower price. This insight encourages consumers to consider older models.

item.title: Legacy TLS tour continues with Exchange Online blocking old versions from July 2026
item.description: Microsoft plans to block legacy TLS versions 1.0 and 1.1 for Exchange Online starting July 2026, urging users to upgrade for better security. This move is part of ongoing efforts to enhance cybersecurity.

item.title: Parallel Web Systems hits $2B valuation five months after its last big raise
item.description: Parallel Web Systems has achieved a $2 billion valuation after raising $100 million, just five months after its last funding round. This reflects the growing interest and investment in AI agent tools.

item.title: Is Facebook adding Gen Z phrases to your shared posts? You're not alone, bestie. Here's what's happening.
item.description: Facebook users have noticed the addition of Gen Z slang in their posts, likely due to AI algorithms. This trend highlights the evolving nature of social media communication.

item.title: Building the compute infrastructure for the Intelligence Age
item.description: OpenAI is scaling its Stargate project to enhance the compute infrastructure necessary for advancing AGI, adding new data center capacity to meet increasing demand. This is a significant step in AI development.

item.title: These two critical Mac security features are off by default - how to turn them on and why you should
item.description: The article warns Mac users about two critical security features that are disabled by default, urging them to enable these settings to protect their devices. This guidance is vital for maintaining security.

item.title: I've used Android Auto for years, and these 5 changes solved my biggest issues
item.description: The article discusses five key changes to Android Auto that enhance user experience during road trips. These adjustments address common frustrations and improve functionality.

item.title: GitHub: Zounds, a genuinely helpful AI-assisted bug report that isn't total slop! Here, Wiz, take this wad of cash
item.description: Wiz researchers discovered a critical flaw in GitHub's infrastructure, allowing attackers to access private repositories. This discovery led to a significant financial reward for the team.

item.title: Asia markets set to open mixed after OPEC shock, tech jitters drag Wall Street stocks
item.description: Asian markets are expected to open mixed as Wall Street stocks fell due to OpenAI's revenue and user growth falling short of expectations. This has raised concerns about the tech sector's stability.

item.title: Bitcoin rally falters as AI industry weakens and CLARITY Act approval odds fall
item.description: Bitcoin's recent rally is stalling due to pressures from the AI industry's downturn, high oil prices, and delays in the CLARITY Act negotiations, impacting investor confidence.

item.title: Is Your Innovation Fire Fading?
item.description: The article explores how innovation often fails due to structural issues rather than a lack of ideas. It emphasizes the need for a supportive environment for innovation to thrive.

item.title: mnemo-claude 0.13.4
item.description: The update to mnemo-claude 0.13.4 introduces features that help users remember information more effectively by integrating with Claude's capabilities.

item.title: BrandJet AI Launches Artemis, a GTM Automation Agent That Manages the Full Sales Workflow End-to-End
item.description: BrandJet AI has launched Artemis, an automation agent designed to streamline the entire sales workflow, enhancing efficiency for businesses.

item.title: Marketers: stop experimenting and start solving real-world problems with AI
item.description: The article advises marketers to focus on solving real-world problems using AI rather than merely experimenting with new technologies, emphasizing practical applications.

item.title: Stedelijk Museum Amsterdam Brings Modern European Art to Samsung Art Store
item.description: Samsung Electronics has collaborated with the Stedelijk Museum Amsterdam to offer a curated selection of modern European art through the Samsung Art Store.

item.title: sage-ai-cli 1.16.42
item.description: Sage AI has released version 1.16.42 of its command-line interface, which focuses on local-first AI coding using open models, promoting user autonomy.

item.title: Superkilonova Cosmic Explosion: A New Stellar Enigma Unveiled - A Square Solutions: Digital Marketing and AI
item.description: A superkilonova cosmic explosion has emerged, challenging current astronomical theories about stellar events. This discovery could reshape our understanding of such cataclysms.

item.title: 'The connective tissue between your data, your people, and your goals': Google Cloud positions Gemini Enterprise as the one-stop shop for all your agentic affairs
item.description: Google Cloud has revamped its Gemini Enterprise platform, introducing new app experiences and an agent builder aimed at enhancing user engagement and productivity.

item.title: OpenAI Joins Amazon Bedrock After New Agreement With Microsoft
item.description: OpenAI's models are now accessible via Amazon Bedrock, enhancing the platform's capabilities for building generative AI applications. This partnership marks a significant development in AI accessibility.

item.title: The Richest Grudge Match in History
item.description: The ongoing trial between Elon Musk and Sam Altman highlights the contentious nature of the AI industry, raising ethical questions about profit motives in AI development.

item.title: atomsci-ampl added to PyPI
item.description: ATOMSCI AMPL, a new open-source Python package, has been added to PyPI, aimed at predicting molecular properties to aid drug discovery efforts.

item.title: AirPods Pro 3 just dropped back down to one of the best prices in months at Amazon
item.description: Amazon has reduced the price of AirPods Pro 3 to one of the lowest in months, making them more accessible to consumers.

item.title: AEO Competitor Analysis: Track AI Answer Engine Rivals
item.description: AEO competitor analysis aims to help marketers understand which competitors appear in AI-generated answers, providing insights into query performance.

item.title: SCOPE-RTM 0.4.1
item.description: The SCOPE-RTM 0.4.1 is a PyTorch reimplementation of the SCOPE radiative transfer model, contributing to advancements in scientific computing.

item.title: Search Live gets ready to follow Gemini with a colorful visual update - Android Authority
item.description: Search Live is preparing to update its interface with a colorful design, following the Gemini platform's enhancements, aimed at improving user experience.

item.title: Musk testifies at OpenAI trial it’s not OK to ‘loot a charity’
item.description: During the OpenAI trial, Musk accused Altman of betraying their original vision by prioritizing profit, igniting discussions on ethics in AI.

item.title: Free Answer Engine Optimization Tools to Benchmark LLM Visibility
item.description: Free answer engine optimization tools can provide valuable data without the need for expensive tech stacks, as demonstrated through extensive testing. This finding is significant for businesses seeking cost-effective solutions for improving their online visibility.

item.title: New unit of measure "Work Life Hours" (WLH) Taxpayer Lifetimes = (D × 1.104) / 2,960,000
item.description: A new metric called 'Work Life Hours' (WLH) has been introduced to quantify taxpayer lifetimes based on project funding. This formula aims to simplify how taxpayer contributions are measured and understood.

item.title: ooai-llm 0.3.1
item.description: The release of ooai-llm 0.3.1 includes new features like typed LLM settings and metadata enrichment, enhancing the functionality of language models. This update is aimed at improving user experience and capabilities in LLM applications.

item.title: deciphercode added to PyPI
item.description: Deciphercode has been added to PyPI, offering a tool for LLM-powered analysis and documentation of legacy code. This tool aims to help developers better understand and manage their codebases.

item.title: Musk kicks off explosive OpenAI trial by slamming Altman, company for abandoning non-profit mission: ‘Not OK to steal a charity’
item.description: Elon Musk's trial against OpenAI began with accusations that CEO Sam Altman transformed the organization from a non-profit into a profit-driven entity. This case raises questions about the ethical direction of AI companies.

item.title: Acquia Wants to Be the Command Center for Your Entire Digital Stack
item.description: Acquia has launched Acquia Source, a platform designed to integrate various digital tools and AI agents while incorporating human oversight. This aims to streamline digital operations for businesses.

item.title: How to do keyword research for AEO (+ Tools)
item.description: The article discusses keyword research for answer engine optimization (AEO), revealing that it differs significantly from traditional SEO methods. This insight is crucial for marketers looking to enhance their content visibility.

item.title: Elon Musk accuses Google co-founder of loving robots as much as people: ‘Larry Page called me a ‘specieist”
item.description: Musk accused Google co-founder Larry Page of being overly fond of robots, using the term 'specieist' to describe Page's views. This reflects Musk's ongoing criticism of AI and its implications.

item.title: Inside the Secret Group Chats Fueling MAGA’s Messaging Machine
item.description: Ashley St. Clair exposed a coordinated effort to shape pro-Trump narratives through secret group chats, revealing the strategic communication methods used in political messaging. This highlights the influence of organized messaging in politics.

item.title: Framework Laptop 16 RTX 5070 12GB upgrade module costs 72% more than the 8GB option
item.description: Framework announced a significant price increase for its RTX 5070 upgrade module, which could affect future pricing of its products. This news is relevant for consumers considering hardware upgrades.
